global_context.scene_description: A vibrant, eye-catching advertising image with a fiery orange and red gradient background suggesting intense summer heat, with subtle flame-like shapes and heat waves throughout. At the very top of the image, large bold white text in a heavy condensed sans-serif font reads 'YOUR AC IS GHOSTING YOU THIS SUMMER.' in all caps. Below the headline, smaller white text reads 'Upgrade before it leaves you sweating.' In the center of the image is a large realistic iPhone shown at a slight angle, displaying an iMessage text conversation. The contact name at the top of the conversation reads 'Your Old AC' with a snowflake emoji. The text bubbles show: a blue outgoing message 'hey it's 110° you coming?' with timestamp '1:15 PM', then another blue outgoing message 'hello??' at '2:45 PM', then another blue outgoing message 'seriously?' at '2:47 PM', and below that a gray 'Read 2:47 PM' receipt. Below the read receipt is a gray typing indicator bubble with three dots, suggesting the AC is struggling to respond. Scattered around the phone are broken red heart emojis and small heat shimmer effects. At the very bottom of the image is a solid bright red horizontal banner spanning full width containing bold white uppercase text reading '$1,400 OFF ANY NEW SYSTEM. SUMMER BLOWOUT.' and the ASH Cooling & Heating logo in white on the right side of the banner.

$1,400 OFF ANY NEW SYSTEM. SUMMER BLOWOUT.

semantic_relationships: Headline text at top establishes the concept immediately
Subline below headline provides context before the eye reaches the phone
iPhone in center IS the visual metaphor — your AC is leaving you on read
The unanswered messages escalate in desperation (casual > confused > frustrated)
Read receipt confirms the AC saw the messages and chose not to respond
Typing indicator suggests the AC is struggling but failing to come through
Broken hearts floating around reinforce the relationship/breakup metaphor
Fiery orange background creates heat atmosphere without being literal
Red banner at bottom contains the offer and brand — the solution to the problem above
